Title: v0.2.7 — Generic .tres mapper + manifest-driven discovery (Genericization Phase 2)
Summary: Second release of the genericization arc. The generic .tres writer is built, validated against real corpus (Karma writeback byte-identical 6/6), and ready to dispatch when projects declare their schemas in the manifest. Manifest-driven discovery is live end-to-end: declare a domain in `bleepforge_manifest.json` and Bleepforge surfaces it in the sidebar with a populated discovery list, reachable from Ctrl+K. Read-only — edit support lands in v0.2.9 once the importer (v0.2.8) closes the round-trip. FoB workflow unchanged.
Body:
## The headline

The v0.2.6 → v0.3.0 arc's riskiest single piece — the **generic .tres mapper** — is built and validated. KarmaImpact + KarmaDelta byte-identical writeback against the real FoB corpus (6/6) proves the generic path handles sub-resource arrays with `_subId` stable matching, default-aware emit, ext-resource minting, and orphan cleanup indistinguishably from the hand-tuned writer it replaces.

It's not yet dispatched in production — FoB's hardcoded writers stay in place via the override registry, and the dispatcher's manifest path waits for v0.2.8's importer + v0.2.9's edit-UI wiring before it does anything user-visible during save. But the writer half of the round-trip is done. v0.2.8 builds the read half. v0.2.9 wires both ends through the dispatcher + a generic edit UI.

**What's user-visible in v0.2.7**: read-only manifest-domain discovery. Open a Godot project whose `bleepforge_manifest.json` declares domains — Bleepforge's sidebar grows a **Manifest** section with one row per declared domain, each linking to a generic list page that shows the discovered `.tres` entities. AppSearch (Ctrl+K) indexes the domains too with a teal **Domain** badge.

Validated end-to-end against `godot-lib/test-project/` which exercises all four entry kinds: `domain` (Note × 3), `foldered` (Snippet × 4), `enumKeyed` (ElementData × 4), `discriminatedFamily` (Equipment × 2 — Sword + Shield variants).

FoB workflow unchanged. FoB's empty manifest means no Manifest section appears + no generic dispatch fires; every existing editor surface works exactly as before.

## What landed in v0.2.7

### Generic .tres mapper — the writer half

A 12-handler dispatch architecture covering every manifest field type. Skeleton + handlers + extResources helper + scriptIndex module + smoke tests + end-to-end harness, all in `server/src/internal/tres/generic/`.

- **Override registry** (`overrideRegistry.ts`) — `registerOverride(domain, fn)` / `getOverride(domain)`. FoB's seven writers register at module init; the unified `writeTres(domain, entity, ctx)` dispatcher consults the registry, then falls through to the generic path.
- **Scalar handlers** (`handlers/{string, numeric, bool, enum}.ts`) — covers string / multiline / flag / int / float / bool / enum. Default-aware emit (omits properties matching the field's declared default or the type's natural default). `showWhen` gating evaluated by the orchestrator before dispatch.
- **Ref handler** (`handlers/ref.ts`) — cross-domain resource references. Resolves target via `WriterContext.resolveRef`, mints `type="Resource"` ext_resources, dedups identical targets.
- **Texture handler** (`handlers/texture.ts`) — Texture2D fields with the AtlasTexture preservation rule ported from `textureRef.ts`. Empty JSON keeps an existing `SubResource(AtlasTexture)` verbatim; non-empty path mints a Texture2D ext_resource and drops the orphaned AtlasTexture sub_resource so its inner atlas ref releases for the orphan cleanup pass.
- **Scene handler** (`handlers/scene.ts`) — PackedScene fields. Accepts either `res://` or absolute path under godotRoot.
- **Array handler** (`handlers/array.ts`) — meatiest single handler. Sub-resource arrays use `reconcileSubResourceArray` with `_subId` stable matching for add / update / remove / reorder; ref arrays mint Resource ext_resources and dedup. Typed-container support (`Array[ExtResource(scriptId)](...)` for sub-resource arrays, `Array[Object](...)` for ref arrays).
- **Subresource handler** (`handlers/subresource.ts`) — single inline sub_resource fields (NPC LootTable shape). Recursive teardown — removing a wrapper also removes nested sub_resources it referenced, so dropping a LootTable cleans up its LootEntries.
- **scriptIndex** (`server/src/lib/scriptIndex/`) — walks `.cs` files + their `.cs.uid` sidecars at boot. Queried by the array + subresource handlers when minting Script ext_resources for new sub_resource sections.

**Validation:** 49 in-process smoke cases (`pnpm --filter @bleepforge/server smoke:generic{,-refs,-arrays,-subresource}`) + the karma byte-identical harness (`smoke:generic-karma`, 6/6 against real FoB corpus). Parser+emitter round-trip harness still 90/90.

See [Generic .tres mapper](/help/under-the-hood/generic-tres-mapper) for the architecture.

### Manifest-driven discovery — the read-only MVP

- **manifestCache** singleton (`server/src/lib/manifest/cache.ts`) — holds the parsed `bleepforge_manifest.json` in memory with O(1) lookups. Built at boot from the same loader the diagnostics endpoint uses; refreshed automatically when the watcher detects manifest changes (godot-lib's Tools-menu re-export, or a hand edit).
- **projectIndex extension** — the runtime singleton that maps every authored Godot resource to its `.tres` file now consumes the manifest cache during its content-classifying walk. All four manifest entry kinds (`domain` / `foldered` / `enumKeyed` / `discriminatedFamily`) get content-driven classification, no hardcoded paths. **Manifest classifiers run BEFORE FoB's hardcoded ones** — the user's manifest is the source of truth for their project; FoB's hardcoded set is a fallback that retires when FoB ports to BleepforgeRegistry.
- **`/api/manifest-domain` endpoints** — index returns the manifest entry list with entity counts; `/:domain` returns the declared Entry + the discovered IndexedTres entries.
- **Routes** — `/manifest` (landing card grid) + `/manifest/:domain` (identity-only entity table). Read-only in v0.2.7.
- **Sidebar** — gains a dynamic **Manifest** section below the FoB nav when the manifest declares any domain. Hidden entirely for FoB-only projects (empty manifest).
- **AppSearch** — manifest domains indexed alongside FoB entities with a teal **Domain** badge.

See [Manifest-driven discovery](/help/under-the-hood/manifest-discovery) for the wiring.

### `[BleepforgeVariantValue]` attribute

Closes the v0.2.6 Phase 5 footnote about variant default-value conflicts. C# inheritance made the manifest emitter's previous "instantiate variant + read default" fallback unreliable — every variant got the base enum's first value as its discriminator. The new attribute (in `addons/bleepforge/runtime/Attributes.cs`) lets variant classes declare their discriminator value explicitly:

```csharp
[GlobalClass, BleepforgeVariantValue("Sword")]
public partial class Sword : Equipment
{
    [Export] public int Damage { get; set; } = 0;
}
```

ManifestEmitter consults the attribute first; the constructor-based workaround still works for back-compat. Test-project's Sword + Shield switched to the attribute and dropped their constructors.

### Bug fixes surfaced by end-to-end testing

- **Restart icon now lights up** when you change godotProjectRoot in Preferences for the active project. Previously the icon only detected project-switch pending state; now it also detects godot-root-change pending state. Click → hot-reload picks up the new path without a full app restart.
- **Classifier order** — manifest classifiers run before FoB hardcoded. Caught during test-project validation: NOTE + EQUIPMENT discovered 0 entities because FoB's "any resource with a Slug field is an item" check was greedy-eating them before they reached the manifest path.

## What's NOT in v0.2.7 (the honest list)

- **Edit support for manifest domains.** The generic `<DomainEdit>` component, per-field-type form renderers, and writeTresGeneric dispatch wiring all land in v0.2.9.
- **Generic importer.** `.tres → JSON` for manifest domains. v0.2.8 — mirror-scale to the writer, plus boot reconcile + watcher reimport hooks.
- **JSON cache for manifest domains.** Today's list endpoint walks projectIndex + returns identity-only payload. No JSON cache means the generic mapper's read side doesn't exist yet. Same v0.2.8 scope.
- **writeTres dispatch through the generic path.** Karma stays on its override; the dispatcher fallthrough is wired but no domain currently exercises it in production.
- **FoB port.** Per the locked v0.2.6 decisions, FoB stays on hand-rolled autoloads through the genericization arc and ports at the end of v0.2.9 / start of v0.3.0. FoB's overrides retire at port time, removing all FoB-specific code from Bleepforge core.

## Workflow note for testing v0.2.7

If you want to point Bleepforge at a manifest-declared project (e.g. `godot-lib/test-project/`), **create a new Bleepforge project** for it rather than repointing an existing project's Godot root. Use `/projects` → **+ New project** → Sync mode → pick the Godot path. Repointing FoB's existing project to a different tree produces confusing integrity errors because FoB's JSON cache references content that doesn't exist in the new tree.

## What's next (v0.2.8)

- **Generic importer** (`server/src/internal/import/generic/`) — `.tres → JSON` covering all 12 field types, mirror of the writer architecture.
- **JSON cache for manifest domains** — `projects/<slug>/data/<domain>/<id>.json`, rebuilt at boot.
- **Boot reconcile extension** — `runBootReconcile` learns to walk manifest domains alongside FoB's hardcoded set.
- **Watcher reimport extension** — `reimportOne` dispatches to the generic importer for manifest-declared `.tres` files.
- **Round-trip harness** — generic equivalent of `pnpm harness`: parse → import → write → emit, compared byte-identical.

v0.2.8 = "manifest domains have a JSON cache, edits in Godot propagate to Bleepforge."

## What's after v0.2.8 (v0.2.9, v0.3.0)

- **v0.2.9** — Edit UI for manifest domains. Generic `<DomainEdit>` driven by manifest field declarations; per-field-type form renderers (text input / textarea / number / checkbox / select / AssetPicker / autocomplete-ref / array editor / inline subresource); `overrideUi` mechanism so bespoke FoB UIs keep working unchanged; FoB port to BleepforgeRegistry at cycle end. After this commit, the FoB override registry is empty and Bleepforge core has zero FoB-specific code.
- **v0.3.0** — Headline cut: "Bleepforge is a generic content studio for any 2D Godot 4 project, or works standalone in notebook mode." Polish, docs pass, the advertise-able release.

> note: the deep dive lives in the **Genericization arc** section of the project's CLAUDE.md. Read there for the locked design decisions across the four-release arc.
